reasoning: The base rate of ICE agents killing U.S. citizens is historically very low, but increased to roughly 2 per year under the recent Trump administration's aggressive enforcement tactics (Ruben Ray Martinez in March 2025 and Renee Good in Jan 2026). The extreme frequency of incidents in January 2026 was largely driven by 'Operation Metro Surge' in Minnesota, which surged thousands of agents into cities. News reports confirm that this operation officially wound down in late February 2026, with the majority of extra agents leaving. Although the market was at 0.17 around mid-February (with 40 days remaining), the conclusion of the operation and the 30% reduction in time remaining (now 31 days left) suggests the probability should scale down proportionately. A Poisson rate of 1-2 incidents per year yields roughly an 8-15% chance in a single month; accounting for the reduced agent footprint, 0.09 reflects the trailing risk.
